REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SPARX 7, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE ENFORCEABLE CONTRACTS BETWEEN THE PARTIES; THE BREACHED TERMS OF EACH CONTRACT; AND BREACH OF THOSE TERMS BY EACH DEFENDANT. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATIONS IS OVERRULED. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS OVERRULED; IT IS NOT DUPLICATIVE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ACCOUNTING IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT ORDINARY LEGAL ACTION DEMANDING A FIXED SUM IS IMPRACTICABLE. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INJUNCTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE INJUNCTION AS A REMEDY FOR A WRONG THAT CANNOT BE COMPENSATED BY MONEY DAMAGES. COMPLAINT IS CURRENTLY SEEKING REPAYMENT OF THE AMOUNTS ALREADY TAKEN FROM THE SOFT-COSTS ACCOUNT. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (501/CFH)

Off Calendar Oct-28-2021 Continued To Nov-16-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:(Zoom appearance)
John Brown, 415.409.8600 of Cornerstone Law Group for Defendant Sparx7, LLC.
No other appearances.

Discussed. The Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT SPARX 7, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 16, 2021. No later than November 5, 2021 Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief limited to the following: (1) citations to cases stating for the proposition that a non-party to a contract is bound by its terms because a party is not capable of performing the terms (See Opposition 6:1-4; TAC 6:15-19); no further argument, this section of the brief shall only include citations or state "none;" (2) facts supporting the allegation that it was the "intention and agreement of the parties that the integration clause in the Ground Lease was to only relate to the Ground Lease terms," specifically facts showing intention and facts showing agreement, e.g. how the intention was communicated, how the agreement was reached, when, by whom, etc.; no further argument, this section of the brief shall only include facts; (3) outline of all breached terms of the agreement(s) of the parties as of December 2017 after the execution of the Ground lease; this outline to be in the table format: Column One - the breached term; Column 2 - source of the term; Column 3- signatories to the document in column 2; Column 4 - specific breach upon which the first cause of action is based (TAC 6:9-7:14), e.g. for the asserted breach by Kevin Lee of "failure to sign the final Agreement to Form Joint Venture, the Construction Rider Work Letter, the Guaranty," (TAC, paragraph 23(a)) what was the term breached, in which document this term appeared and who are the signatories to that document as it appears on the document; no further argument for this part of the brief; The Court will not consider the supplemental brief if any part of it is not responsive to the request outlined in this ruling. Mr. Brown to give notice. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

John C. Brown; Cornerstone Law Group; 415-409-8600; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Sparx7, LLC and Kevin Lee.
Charles S. Bronitsky; Law Office of Charles S. Bronitsky; 650-918-5760; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs San Pablo SR2, LLC and San Palbo SR3, LLC.
Kevin Lee; defendant, in attendance via Zoom.

Discussed. The court asks defendant Kevin Lee if he has ever worked for the San Francisco Superior Court. Kevin Lee responds that he has never worked for the courts. The court explains that a person named Kevin Lee used to work for the San Francisco Superior Court, and that Mr. Lee was asked to attend today's hearing so that it could be determined whether or not the judge would need to recuse himself from this matter. After hearing discussions the court rules as follows: DEFENDANTS SPARX 7, LLC and KEVIN LEE'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege (1) a contract with Kevin Lee in his individual capacity and facts in support of each element of that cause of action against Kevin Lee; (2) facts in support of each element of breach of contract as to the other defendant identifying the contract; to the extent breach is based on the Term Sheet, allege facts, not conclusions rendering the integration clause in the Ground Lease ineffective; and (3) allege facts supporting "who, when, to whom, and how" as to the misrepresentations - general allegations that multiple defendants made multiple representations both in wringing and orally to multiple plaintiffs are not sufficient. Amended Complaint to be filed within 30 days from service of notice of entry of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANT SPARX 7, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

Complaint is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with leave to amend to allege enforceable contracts between the parties; the breached terms of each contract; and breach of those terms by each defendant. Demurrer to the causes of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentations is overruled. These causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is overruled; it is not duplicative of the cause of action for breach of contract. Demurrer to the cause of action for accounting is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that ordinary legal action demanding a fixed sum is impracticable. Demurrer to the cause of action for injunction is sustained with leave to amend to allege injunction as a remedy for a wrong that cannot be compensated by money damages. Complaint is currently seeking repayment of the amounts already taken from the Soft-Costs Account. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Order is signed. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not reported (501/CFH)
